nodes.label: CSP Drift Guard Pattern
nodes.rationale: vercel.json must match canonical CSP from securityHeaders.ts; parity test fails the build if they diverge, preventing the class of bug where documented policy and deployed policy diverge

nodes.label: PRF-v1 Vault Key Wrapping Scheme
nodes.rationale: Vault key is wrapped with HKDF-SHA256-derived key from WebAuthn PRF output. PRF output never stored — XSS or full IndexedDB dump cannot re-derive wrapping key. Previous device-key scheme was insecure because inputs (credentialId, userId, salt) were all stored and recomputable.

nodes.label: S7 Non-Extractable Session Key
nodes.rationale: unwrapVaultKeyWithPRF takes raw vault key bytes (not a CryptoKey) so the session CryptoKey can stay non-extractable; transient raw bytes zeroized in finally block after import

nodes.label: PBKDF2 600k Iterations OWASP 2025
nodes.rationale: PBKDF2_ITERATIONS=600000 per OWASP 2025 guidelines; deriveKeyFromPassword imports as non-extractable WebCrypto key and zeroizes transient derived bytes afterward (S7)

nodes.label: Self-Hosted OCR Assets Pattern
nodes.rationale: Tesseract assets served from public/ocr/ (self-hosted, same-origin) instead of cdn.jsdelivr.net, eliminating CDN supply chain risk (P2). Assets pinned via package-lock.json and copied by scripts/copy-ocr-assets.js at predev/prebuild.

nodes.label: S5 Sealed Metadata Pattern
nodes.rationale: All sensitive credential metadata (title, username, url, tags, card fields) stored encrypted with vault key. Only non-identifying index fields (category, isFavorite, timestamps) remain plaintext. Pre-v5 records upgraded by sealLegacyMetadata().

nodes.label: S4 Rehash-on-Login Pattern
nodes.rationale: Transparently upgrades legacy/weak scrypt password hashes to current parameters on successful login. Best-effort: rehash failure must never block login.

nodes.label: S1 PRF-Only Biometric Invariant
nodes.rationale: Only PRF-scheme (vaultKeyScheme='prf-v1') credentials can unlock vault. stripLegacyBiometric removes legacy credentials on each password login. Enrollment requires WebAuthn PRF extension; if authenticator does not enable PRF, enrollment is rejected.

nodes.label: S8 Schema Validation Before Repository
nodes.rationale: Every vault import validated against Zod schema BEFORE any row touches the repository. Malformed, oversized, or wrong-shaped input rejected with user-friendly error. Unknown keys stripped (not rejected) for forward compatibility with future export versions.

nodes.label: PRF Tri-State Support Detection (unknown/supported/unsupported)
nodes.rationale: Only blocks or warns when PRF is *known* unsupported — not when merely unverifiable — to avoid false negatives on browsers where getClientCapabilities is unavailable

nodes.label: Master Password Zeroized After Enrollment Attempt
nodes.rationale: Never retain the password in state longer than the enrollment attempt — S7 key hygiene applied to transient form state

nodes.label: PRF-Wrapped Vault Key Not Decryptable With Master Password
PRF Unwrap Produces Non-Extractable CryptoKey (S7 test)
Vault Key Never Persisted in Storage Test
Workbox OCR Assets Runtime Cache (CacheFirst, no precache)
nodes.rationale: OCR assets (~16MB) are runtime-cached on first OCR use, not precached, to avoid bloating the precache budget

nodes.label: Tesseract OCR Assets Self-Hosted Under /ocr/ (P2)
nodes.rationale: P2: OCR assets served from public/ocr/ to eliminate cdn.jsdelivr.net supply-chain exposure and remove CDN origins from CSP

nodes.label: Dev Security Headers (DEV_SECURITY_HEADERS) — Relaxed for HMR
Preview Security Headers (SECURITY_HEADERS) — Strict Production Policy
Sentinel Log: XSS Fix in BreachDetailsModal via DOMPurify
S1: WebAuthn PRF Zero-Knowledge Biometric Unlock
nodes.rationale: The PRF extension makes biometric unlock a true cryptographic gate. The wrap key is derived from an authenticator secret (never stored) via HKDF-SHA256, so neither XSS nor an IndexedDB dump can unlock the vault. Replaces the insecure device-key scheme where inputs were all stored and recomputable. Scheme: vaultKeyScheme='prf-v1'

nodes.label: S2: Strict Hash-Based CSP (no unsafe-inline/unsafe-eval in script-src)
nodes.rationale: script-src uses SHA-256 hash of the single inline bootstrap script plus wasm-unsafe-eval, eliminating unsafe-inline/unsafe-eval. style-src unsafe-inline is a documented residual for MUI/Emotion. A drift test recomputes the hash from index.html on every test run.

nodes.label: S7: Non-Extractable Session Vault Keys + Key Material Zeroization
nodes.rationale: Session vault keys are non-extractable CryptoKeys on both unlock paths. Transient raw key material (PBKDF2 output, decrypted vault-key bytes, PRF outputs) is zeroized (.fill(0)) immediately after use. Biometric enrollment confirms master password and recovers vault key from encrypted storage — the in-memory session key is never exported.

nodes.label: S8: Zod-Validated Vault Imports
nodes.rationale: Vault imports are schema-validated with Zod (entry cap, field caps, category/cardType enums, type checks) before any row is processed, preventing malformed or oversized import payloads

nodes.label: P2: Tesseract OCR Assets Self-Hosted (No CDN Egress)
nodes.rationale: Tesseract OCR assets are self-hosted under public/ocr/ (version-pinned via package-lock), eliminating the former cdn.jsdelivr.net supply-chain exposure. No CDN origins remain in the CSP.

nodes.label: P5: Dead Dependencies Removed (argon2-browser, dexie-encrypted)
nodes.rationale: argon2-browser (bundled WASM) and dexie-encrypted (beta) removed to reduce attack surface, bundle weight, and eliminate the historic reason unsafe-eval was tolerated

nodes.label: HKDF-SHA256 Vault Key Wrap Key Derivation from PRF Output
Scrypt Password Hashing (N=32768, r=8, p=1) via @noble/hashes
M1 Fix: IndexedDB-Backed Rate Limiter with Exponential Backoff
L0 Fix: Generator Preferences Removed from localStorage (CWE-312)
Hardening Phase A: Remove Dead Dependencies (P5)
Hardening Phase B: Self-Host Tesseract OCR Assets (P2)
Hardening Phase C: Strict Hash-Based CSP (S2)
Hardening Phase D: Key Hygiene - Non-Extractable Keys (S7)
Hardening Phase E: S8 Remainder + Documentation Sync
Supply Chain First Phase Order Rationale
nodes.rationale: Supply chain hardening (A, B) runs first because removing cdn.jsdelivr.net and dead deps shrinks the CSP allowlist, making the strict CSP (C) simpler and testable in one pass. Key hygiene (D) runs alone with integration-test gate.

nodes.label: Security & PWA Enhancement Plan Status Table (2026-06-10)
S1 Critical Finding: Biometric Unlock Not a Cryptographic Boundary (Original)
S2 High Finding: Deployed CSP Uses unsafe-inline/unsafe-eval (Original)
Roadmap Security Hardening Phases A-E Summary (2026-06-10)
Roadmap S1 WebAuthn PRF Vault Unlock Note
Test Status: S1 WebAuthn PRF Tests (May 30, 2026)
index.html GitHub Pages SPA Redirect Inline Script
index.html Viewport Meta (WCAG 1.4.4 — User Scaling Enabled)
CLAUDE.md: argon2-browser Removed, Using @noble/hashes/scrypt
nodes.rationale: argon2-browser removed due to CSP/WASM loading issues. Crypto now uses @noble/hashes/scrypt to avoid the need for unsafe-eval in script-src

nodes.label: CLAUDE.md: Security Headers via HTTP Not Meta Tags
nodes.rationale: Set via HTTP headers only, never meta tags. Headers defined in src/config/securityHeaders.ts, single source of truth shared with vercel.json
